text.title: Onboarding Agent Rules and Antipatterns
text.summary: Calendar conflict risks, privacy constraints, localization issues, and agent-specific failure modes.

antipattern.description: Including "review company values" or "explore documentation" without linking to a specific page or resource.
antipattern.reason: Generic tasks are busywork that new hires complete by opening a random wiki page. Every task must point to a named resource; agents should fail loudly if the resource link is missing from the input context.

rule.statement: Never auto-schedule calendar invites without reading freebusy data first. Always check for conflicts before generating an .ics file or sending invites.
rule.rationale: Auto-scheduled invites without freebusy checks produce double-bookings on Day 1, creating a bad first impression that is difficult to recover from.

rule.statement: Day 1 agenda: working laptop, accounts configured, lunch with team. No heavy training modules on Day 1.
rule.rationale: First impressions are sticky. A new hire who spends Day 1 watching compliance videos and waiting for IT access remembers that experience. Functional setup + human connection on Day 1 drives 30-day satisfaction scores.

rule.statement: Onboarding plans may contain manager-only information (project codenames, customer names, team rosters). Enforce visibility boundaries — do not include restricted information in new-hire-visible onboarding documents.
rule.rationale: New hires do not have the same information access as managers on Day 1. Including restricted details in their onboarding doc creates an unauthorized disclosure.

rule.statement: HRIS data sent to LLM prompts for onboarding plan generation must comply with GDPR and local data protection rules. Do not send personal data across jurisdictions without verified legal basis.
rule.rationale: Personal data (name, role, manager, project assignment) crossing borders into LLM API calls may require data processing agreements not yet in place for new-hire data.

antipattern.description: Applying US-style "celebrate Day 1" high-energy onboarding tone to DE/JP/UA new hires without localization.
antipattern.reason: Onboarding formality norms differ significantly across cultures. An overly casual, celebrations-heavy tone lands awkwardly in markets where professional distance is expected in the first weeks.

text.reference: Agent entry point: generate plan from inputs and create GitHub issue for manager.
